More Notes: Probability of spawning biomass dropping below 20% of unfished level in 2099 is used as a conservation metric in the analysis.
Catches equilibrate at close to 170,000 t when assuming the null environmental model.

All environmental scenarios, even the more optimistic ones (i.e., ssp126), result in a spawning biomass below the target level of 40% of the unfished level by the end of the projection period. Interpretation: value is greater than 60 because 40 is the target, and the level is 'below' the target, which indicates >=60% depletion (below 40% remaining). This result reflects model outputs rather than observations.

Climate effects in the scenarios with the highest warming rates exceed the benefit of the harvest control rule by the end of the century (under GFDL, and MIROC ssp585 forcing scenarios). The NPFMC 40–5 harvest control rule attenuates biomass declines relative to a constant FMEY harvest control rule through reduced catch.
